# Narrator Convergence Plan

## Stage Path
1. `distant_observer`
2. `lineage_aware_guide`
3. `emotionally_invested_interpreter`
4. `family_near_witness`
5. `inherited_memory_carrier`
6. `threshold_of_self`
7. `first_person_presence`

## Trigger Chain
- Approaching grandfather line
- Approaching father line
- Approaching self-line
- Direct memory threshold
- Direct witness threshold
- Identity/lineage recognition threshold

## Mode Transition Rules
- No leap larger than two presence bands without explicit multi-trigger support.
- First-person transition requires witness and identity threshold evidence.
- Era shifts require narrator continuity anchor cues in opening bridge windows.

## Era Distance Rules
- Distant eras bias subtle/guiding/reflective presence.
- Family-near eras bias personal/intimate presence.
- Self-era threshold can move to first-person only when trigger chain validates.

## Boundary Rules
- Narrator framing cannot overwrite character-era cognition.
- Reflective authority remains evidence-bound.
- Cultural and historical specificity is preserved even when narrator intimacy rises.
